page: Microsoft Azure B2as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 2 vCPUs, derived from 1 physical core and 2 threads. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance under stress-ng is rated as strong, and single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance. However, multi-core performance is weak, as reflected in both stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory performance is a bottleneck, with Passmark memory scores and small-block read-only memory bandwidth falling into the poor category. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and software compilation also register in the bottom tier of benchmarks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the burstable CPU allocation model makes this instance a resource-dense option for workloads with variable CPU utilization. It avoids the higher cost of continuously allocated compute resources by allowing users to pay for a lower baseline performance with the ability to burst when needed. Due to its limited memory bandwidth and weak multi-core scaling, it is not suited for intensive database hosting, heavy compilation, or complex rendering. Instead, it is best fit for low-traffic web serving, light text processing, small-scale development environments, and basic background utility tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure B2as_v2 is an x86_64 burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, but weak multi-core scaling and poor memory bandwidth. Consequently, database operations, software compilation, and Redis throughput are limited. This instance offers a cost-effective profile for workloads with variable CPU demands due to its burstable allocation model. It is best suited for light workloads such as low-traffic web serving, basic text processing, and development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B2as_v2 is a burstable x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7763 processor and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B2as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, suited for light, variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable, dual-vCPU virtual machine with AMD EPYC processing and balanced memory for cost-effective, variable-demand workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
Configured with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage
Burstable CPU allocation for cost-efficient handling of variable workloads
Strong single-core performance contrasted with weak multi-core scaling
Best suited for low-traffic web serving, light text processing, and development environments
